description: This figure presents the microstructural evolution of Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% low-density steel under different processing conditions, with all subgraphs sharing a 20 μm scale bar. Subgraph (a) is an optical micrograph of the as-cast sample, showing δ-ferrite dendrites distributed in the γ-austenite matrix. Subgraphs (b) and (c) are optical micrographs of hot-rolled (HR) and cold-rolled (CR) samples separately, demonstrating that δ-ferrite transforms into lamellar B2 stringer bands after HR, and the B2 bands are further thinned with γ grains flattened into pancake shape after CR. Subgraph (d) is a field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM) image of the HR sample, exhibiting intragranular B2 (IG-B2) precipitates nucleated at shear bands within the γ matrix. These observations confirm that thermomechanical processing can effectively regulate the phase morphology and distribution of the studied steel, laying a microstructural foundation for optimizing its mechanical properties.
